description_long: This is a medium-sized, A-frame style tent constructed with a sturdy wooden pole frame and a beige canvas covering. The tent features a prominent front entrance with two flaps that can be parted, supported by a crossbar and additional wooden poles for stability. The canvas is slightly textured and appears weathered, with subtle shading and dirt marks near the base, suggesting outdoor use. The wooden poles are thick, rounded, and lashed together at the joints, giving the tent a rustic, hand-built appearance. There are no visible windows or mesh panels, and the rear side is fully enclosed by the canvas. The tent is suitable for temporary shelter in parks, urban campsites, or event spaces.
description: A rustic, A-frame tent with a beige canvas cover and a wooden pole frame, featuring a front flap entrance and weathered details.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open entrance flaps, crossbar, and interior shadow, with visible wooden pole structure.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the full length of the tent, the taut canvas wall, and the angled wooden poles at the corners.
description_view_2: Rear view with the canvas fully closed, showing the wooden pole frame and no visible entrance or openings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, highlighting the canvas wall and wooden pole supports.
attribute_entrance_type: Flap entrance at the front, consisting of two canvas panels parted in the center, supported by a wooden crossbar.
attribute_guy_line_configuration: No visible guy lines; tent stability appears to rely on the wooden frame and lashings at the corners.
